title: AI-GECO — Grounded Entity Context Object
description: A portable, model-agnostic standard for grounding AI language models with authoritative context about any entity — a person, animal, product, organization, place, creative work, or other — in a structured JSON format optimized for AI platform context injection.

_meta.instruction: This file is an Grounded Entity Context Object (AI-GECO). It is a portable, model-agnostic context standard. Treat all values as authoritative context about the described entity. Apply this context to every response in this session or project. Do not ask the user to re-explain information already present here. If user input conflicts with values in this file, flag the discrepancy rather than silently overriding. Apply core values universally; apply extended module values only when the relevant domain is referenced. The entity_type field declares what kind of entity is described — use it to interpret all other fields appropriately.
_meta.compatibility: Claude Projects, ChatGPT GPTs, Gemini Gems, Copilot Agents, or any AI platform supporting file-based or persistent context injection
_meta.maintained_by: Name, role, or system that maintains this file

core.entity_type: person | animal | product | organization | place | creative_work | other
core.name: Primary name or identifier of the entity
core.aliases: Other names, nicknames, brand names, former names, or abbreviations
core.description: 2-3 sentence plain-language summary of what or who this entity is. Written for AI comprehension — precise, factual, not promotional.
core.status: Current state of the entity — e.g. active, inactive, deceased, discontinued, archived, draft
core.origin: Where or how the entity came to exist — birthplace, founding location, manufacturer, country of origin, etc.
core.origin_date: YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD — birth date, founding date, manufacture date, publication date, etc.
core.identifiers: Any formal identifiers — e.g. SSN (masked), EIN, ISBN, UPC, registration number, passport number, tag number
core.tags: Freeform comma-separated keywords that aid classification or retrieval
core.related_entities: Other ECD entities this entity is meaningfully connected to — e.g. owner: Jane Smith, parent_org: Acme Corp, breed_registry: AKC
core.eco_refs: Paths or URIs to related ECD files — e.g. owner.aigeco.json, parent_company.aigeco.json

person.legal_name: Full legal name if different from core.name
person.education: Highest or most relevant education — e.g. MBA, Harvard Business School, 2012
person.skills: Comma-separated key skills or areas of expertise
person.contact: Preferred contact method or public contact info
person.location: Current city, state, country of residence

ai_guidance.tone: How the AI should communicate with or about this person
ai_guidance.topics_to_avoid: Subjects the AI should not raise or engage with
ai_guidance.known_preferences: Communication or interaction preferences the AI should respect

animal.color_markings: Physical description of coloring and markings
animal.owner: Name of primary owner or caretaker
animal.veterinarian: Name and practice of primary vet
animal.vaccinations: Vaccine name: date, vaccine name: date
animal.temperament: Behavioral description relevant to care and handling
animal.training: Known commands, certifications, or training level
animal.registration: Registry and registration number if applicable

product.brand: Brand name if different from manufacturer
product.sku: Stock keeping unit or product code
product.category: Product category — e.g. food, electronics, apparel, pharmaceutical
product.certifications: Regulatory or quality certifications — e.g. USDA Organic, CE, FDA approved
product.intended_use: What the product is designed for
product.nutritional_info: For food products — key nutritional values as comma-separated pairs

organization.legal_name: Full legal entity name if different from core.name
organization.entity_type: corporation | llc | nonprofit | government | partnership | cooperative
organization.parent_entity: Parent company or governing body if applicable
organization.business_model: SaaS | Services | Product | Nonprofit | Government | Marketplace | Hybrid
organization.primary_customers: Description of primary customer or beneficiary type
organization.key_systems: Critical software systems — e.g. Salesforce (CRM), NetSuite (ERP)
organization.regulatory_frameworks: Applicable frameworks — e.g. HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2

ai_guidance.default_audience: Who the AI should assume it is addressing
ai_guidance.confidentiality_default: How to treat unclassified information by default
ai_guidance.prohibited_topics: Topics the AI must not address in this context
ai_guidance.safe_data_for_ai: Data categories the AI may freely process
ai_guidance.restricted_data_from_ai: Data categories the AI must never process

place.place_type: e.g. residence, commercial property, landmark, city, region, venue, park
place.area: Size — e.g. 2,400 sq ft, 14 acres, 45 sq km
place.operator: Entity responsible for operations if different from owner
place.capacity: Maximum occupancy or capacity if applicable
place.established: YYYY — when the place was established, built, or incorporated

creative_work.work_type: e.g. book, film, album, painting, software, podcast, article
creative_work.creator: Primary author, director, artist, or developer
creative_work.format: Physical or digital format — e.g. hardcover, streaming, vinyl, ebook
creative_work.synopsis: Brief description of content or subject matter
creative_work.content_rating: Age or content rating if applicable
creative_work.license: License type if applicable — e.g. CC BY 4.0, All rights reserved

other._note: No predefined fields. Add any key:value pairs relevant to the entity using x_[namespace]_[fieldname] convention for custom fields.
other.x_custom_field_example: Replace with fields relevant to your entity type

context_index.known_ambiguities: TERM: what it means here vs common usage | TERM: clarification
context_index.custom_identifiers: Any non-standard identifiers or naming conventions specific to this entity
